description: A release.yml step used 'if: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN != "" }}' to gate the deferred npm-publish; GitHub rejected the ENTIRE workflow at parse time ('context secrets is not allowed here'), so the rc7 tag's release run failed before any job started — and plain YAML parse + the wave's adversarial review both MISSED it
resolution: Map the secret into a JOB-LEVEL env var (env: { NPM_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }} }) then gate the step on 'if: ${{ env.NPM_TOKEN != "" }}' (env IS allowed in if:). Root prevention: run actionlint over .github/workflows/*.yml in CI (added, pinned v1.7.7) — it catches context-availability errors that YAML parse and human review miss. Verified: actionlint flags the secrets-in-if form and passes the env-indirection form. The downloadable artifact lesson also recurred: 'main green' != 'release succeeds' != 'binary ships the feature' — only cutting the tag + downloading the binary + running 'am setup --help' proved rc7 fixed the 288-commit-stale gap.
